Creating Prelaunch Content, Part 1
!
This video is all about creating pre-launch content.
!
It's about the different types of pre-launch content and the tools that we use to create
the pre-launch content.
!
A few things that are important to remember to start with is the term “horses for
courses”. I think of it as an Australian term. This is the idea that different horses are
better for different race courses and different types of pre-launched content are right
for different situations.
!
Just because you see someone doing a launch with some certain type of pre-launch
content doesn't mean you have to do it that way. Just because you see me using some
fancy video, it doesn't mean you have to do a fancy video. It's all about what's right for
your situation and what you use depends on your situation and your assets which you
can pull off. These days, I have got a video studio, I've got a bunch of great video
equipment. I've got a great videographer and director/producer who also happens to be
my son, Daniel.
!
Five years ago, I didn't have any of those assets and it was really difficult for me to do
full motion videos, so I did a lot less of it. Another thing you can do is a launch without
using any video whatsoever but video really is great for a lots of situations. The reason
is because you will do best, your launch will do best if with human face. In other words,
no one wants to buy from some big corporation. You want to feel connected to the
people they're buying from. You should never feel worried about presenting some of
your personality within your marketing.
!
In fact, that's one of the things that has set me apart right from the very beginning is I
didn't try to look like some big corporation. Even way back in 1996, 1997 when I was
getting started and everyone was trying to appear like some big fancy corporation, I
just intuitively felt that it would be better if I just spoken in more personal warm voice
and people realized I was a real human. Nowadays, a lot more people realize that and
we see even the largest companies trying to appear a lot more human.
!
Frankly, that's one of the advantages most of us have. The people watching this tend to
be 	in 	smaller 	businesses, 	sometimes 	even 	micro, 	tiny 	businesses. 	One 	of 	the
advantages we have over large companies is that we're very human and that people
can really build a connection with us. Now the thing is, when you are going to seek that
connection, you want to be warm and personable, and you want to have a personality.
You don't want to have some stuffy airlines speaking. When you're going to board a
plane, they always talk to you like not even a human.
!
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 2
PLF_Module4v1.0

-- 2 of 4 --

You want to avoid that at all cost and a lot of times when people start writing for
instance and they might try write a report for part of their pre-launch. They might try to
write a blog post, a lot of times for people their writing.They'll become a lot more stuffy
and 	corporate 	sounding 	or 	sound 	like 	a 	college 	professor. 	It's 	a 	lot 	less 	human
sounding, a lot less personable, less like a real person. It's easy for many of us to fall
into that trap once we start writing.
!
Turn a video camera on and maybe the first few times it will be awkward but our
humanity just instantly shines through when we do video for most of us. That's why, I
would encourage you to consider using video even if you're not terribly comfortable.
!
We'll talk about that in just a minute but your personality shines through with video
and people can easily tell you're a human. A lot of ways for non-professionals, for
people that aren't great marketers, great sales people, it's often easier to connect with
your audience, connect with your prospects in a lot more personable and human way
when you're using video as supposed to using text.
!
Remember, it's all about progress, it's not about perfection. Every launch I do, I get
better at. If you look at the last launch I did, it is a 100% better than the launch I did a
year ago. If you took a look at what I look like when I first started doing video, it wasn't
a very pretty sight. I just keep on getting better and getting better, and you know what,
my next launch will be even better. It's about progress not perfection. You will get
better at.
!
Let's take a quick overview of some of the tapes of pre-launched content.
!
First of all, we have screen cap video. Screen cap videos actually what you're looking
at right now. Basically, I'm using software to capture what's happening on my computer
screen. I'm sitting in my office, I've got a presentation put together and I'm just
recording my screen while I talk into a microphone very simple, easy to do, and it can
be very, very effective.
!
Then you could use what we called full motion or live video. This is regular video
like you would see on TV. This is where you turn the camera on and start recording
yourself, that's full motion or live video. Also very, very effective in terms of pre-
launched content.
!
Two very different types of video but both are very, very powerful. Both have strengths
and/or positives and minuses.
!
There's also audio, something we used to use way back in the dark ages maybe
around 2000, 2001, 2, 3, 4 until video start again easier probably around 2007, 2008.
We use to hear a lot of audio, don't do so much of it anymore.
!

-- 3 of 4 --

PDF reports, where you actually put together some type of written materials, stuff
that can go into a PDF. It doesn't have to be written, it could be mind maps and other
stuff like that. We'll get into that more later on.
!
PDF's, I have a long history of using those in product launches. In fact, I think I've
probably used a PDF report in just about every launch I've done in the last eight, nine
years.
!
Then there's webinars and teleseminars, they can be very, very powerful. Don't
have to be overly complex but they can be great. They can definitely be used as great
pre-launched content. Also great once you open cart and you're actually selling your
product. There is software, and this is one I don't have a lot of experience in but it's
been used very effectively.
!
I'm sure there's other types of pre-launched content, I haven't thought about, we
haven't discovered yet or I'm forgetting about but those are the primary ones right
there. In my next video, we'll start going through all these different types starting with
screen capture video and full motion video.
